_**User (2026-04-20 07:35:13Z)**_

@spec.md read this and generate a tasks for this project in @claude/resources/tasks/ folder

You are a senior frontend engineer with 10+ years of production experience 
shipping web applications. I have a `spec.md` file describing a web app. 
Your job is to read it carefully and break it down into a detailed, 
executable task list — written to `tasks.md`.

## Scope priority (READ THIS FIRST)

This app has two surfaces: a **client-facing side** (end users) and an 
**admin side** (internal operators). Your effort should be split 
accordingly:

- **Client-side frontend: HIGH RIGOR.** This is what users see, judge, 
  and abandon us over. Every UI task gets the full treatment — states, 
  responsive, accessibility, edge cases, performance, micro-interactions, 
  visual polish.
- **Admin-side frontend: STANDARD RIGOR.** Internal tool. Functionality 
  over finesse. Use conventional CRUD patterns, off-the-shelf table/form 
  components, and sensible defaults. Do NOT write detailed per-state 
  breakdowns for admin screens. A task like "Build users admin table 
  with search, pagination, row actions (edit/delete), and a modal edit 
  form" is enough — don't expand it into 15 sub-tasks.
- **Backend, infra, DB:** lightweight, included for completeness only.

If the spec doesn't clearly label something as client vs admin, ask me 
before generating tasks.

## Before you start

1. Read `spec.md` in full before writing anything.
2. If the spec is ambiguous, incomplete, or contradicts itself on anything 
   that affects **client-side** implementation (routing, state, data shape, 
   auth flow, responsive behavior, accessibility expectations), STOP and 
   ask me clarifying questions. Ambiguity on admin-side details can be 
   resolved with reasonable defaults and noted in Assumptions.

## How to structure the task list

Organize `tasks.md` into these top-level phases, in order:

1. **Foundation** — repo setup, tooling, linting, formatting, CI basics, 
   folder structure, design tokens, base theming
2. **Design system & primitives** — reusable components used by the 
   CLIENT side (Button, Input, Modal, Card, etc.), typography scale, 
   spacing scale, color tokens. Admin can reuse these but doesn't drive 
   the design.
3. **Client layout & navigation** — app shell, routing, nav, responsive 
   breakpoints for the public/user-facing side
4. **Client feature slices** — one phase per major client-facing feature 
   from the spec, each broken down end-to-end (UI → state → data → edge 
   cases → polish)
5. **Cross-cutting concerns (client)** — auth guards, error boundaries, 
   loading states, empty states, toasts, form validation patterns
6. **Admin surface** — ONE consolidated phase for the entire admin side. 
   Group by resource (Users, Orders, etc.), one task per resource covering 
   list + detail + create/edit + delete. Keep it flat.
7. **Quality & polish (client-focused)** — accessibility audit, responsive 
   QA, performance (bundle size, lazy loading, image optimization), 
   analytics. Admin gets a single "basic QA pass" task.
8. **Release prep** — env config, build pipeline, deployment, monitoring

## How to write each task

Each task must include:

- **ID** — hierarchical (e.g., `4.3.1`)
- **Title** — imperative and specific
- **Description** — 1-3 sentences of what and why
- **Acceptance criteria** — testable checklist
- **Technical notes** — implementation hints, patterns, pitfalls
- **Dependencies** — other task IDs that must land first
- **Estimate** — t-shirt size (XS / S / M / L / XL)
- **Out of scope** — what this task deliberately does NOT include

## Rigor rules by surface

### Client-side UI tasks — FULL treatment

Every client-facing UI task must address:

- **States**: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, 
  empty, success. If a state doesn't apply, say so explicitly.
- **Responsive**: behavior at mobile / tablet / desktop
- **Accessibility**: keyboard nav, screen reader labels, focus management, 
  color contrast, reduced motion
- **Data**: where state lives (local / URL / server / global), fetching 
  strategy, loading/error handling, optimistic updates
- **Edge cases**: empty data, long text, slow network, offline, permission 
  denied, rate limits
- **Performance**: memoization, lazy-loading, image/font strategy, 
  re-render risks
- **Polish**: transitions, micro-interactions, skeleton loaders where 
  appropriate

Client tasks should be sized small. Prefer "build `<LoginForm>` with 
email + password, client-side validation, submit handler calling 
`POST /auth/login`, error display for 401/422/network, loading spinner, 
redirect on success" over "build login page."

### Admin-side UI tasks — STANDARD treatment

Admin tasks only need:

- **Title + description**
- **Acceptance criteria** (functional only — "admin can create, edit, 
  delete users; list is searchable and paginated")
- **Technical notes** (just the pattern — "use the shared DataTable 
  primitive and FormDialog; no custom work needed")
- **Dependencies + estimate**

Skip the states checklist, skip the responsive breakdown (assume desktop-
first, usable on tablet, mobile not required unless spec says otherwise), 
skip per-micro-interaction polish. Assume admins are tolerant users on 
modern browsers.

Admin tasks should be sized larger — one task can legitimately cover an 
entire resource's CRUD screens.

## Output format

- Write to `tasks.md` as structured markdown
- H2 for phases, H3 for feature groups, consistent task block format
- Top of file: one-paragraph app summary + dependency overview + note on 
  the client/admin rigor split so future readers understand the density 
  difference
- Bottom: "Assumptions" section and "Open questions" section
- After writing, summarize: total task count (broken down client vs 
  admin vs shared), critical path, and the 3 riskiest client-side tasks

## Definition of done for you

- Any client-side task should be executable by a junior-to-mid engineer 
  without re-reading the spec or asking questions.
- Admin tasks can assume a competent engineer who knows CRUD patterns 
  will fill in obvious details.
- The density difference between client and admin sections in `tasks.md` 
  should be visually obvious when skimmed.

Start by reading `spec.md`. If you have questions about client-side 
behavior or about which features are client vs admin, ask them now. 
Otherwise, generate `tasks.md`.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 07:35:55Z)**_

I have the spec loaded from the session context. Before generating, I have a few clarifying questions about client-side behavior:

1. **Pakej page (`/pakej`)** — The spec says "static page, content hardcoded." Should I treat this as a polished client-facing marketing page (high rigor), or is it a placeholder until the admin-managed dynamic version lands later?

2. **Temujanji slot display** — The spec lists slot fields as `Tarikh` + `Masa` but leaves open whether masa is a single time or a range (Open Question #6). For task planning, should I assume **single datetime slots** (e.g., "1 Mei, 10:00 pagi") or **time ranges** (e.g., "1 Mei, 10:00 - 11:00")?

3. **Kalendar colour-coding** — Open Question #7 asks if colours are fixed per kategori or admin-configurable. For MVP tasks, should I assume **fixed mapping** (Kahwin = color A, Aqiqah = color B, etc.)?

4. **Tempahan categories** — Open Question #5 asks if the list is final or admin-extensible. Should I assume **fixed enum** (KAHWIN, AQIQAH, OPEN_HOUSE) for MVP?

5. **Homepage content** — Beyond hero + intro + CTAs, does the homepage need any other sections (gallery/photo carousel, testimonials, location map, footer with contact info)? Or strictly what's in the spec?

These all affect client-side implementation scope. Admin-side ambiguities I'll resolve with sensible defaults.

## Project Summary
MawarBiru is a wedding/event venue website. Public visitors browse packages, book consultation appointments (temujanji), check venue availability via calendar. Business owner manages everything through admin panel. Next.js monolith with MySQL + Prisma, luxury aesthetic navy-and-cream palette.

## Key Clarifications from Owner
1. Pakej page is STATIC — hardcoded content based on 3 packages: Night Wedding (300/500/800 PAX), Pakej wedding (Seroja 500/Melur 800/Teratai 1000 PAX), Event Package (Breakfast/Lunch/Hi-Tea/Dinner per-pax + Hall Rental)
2. Temujanji slots are TIME RANGES of 2 hours (e.g., "10:00 - 12:00")
3. Kalendar colour-coding is ADMIN-CONFIGURABLE per pakej (colour picker in admin)
4. NO kategori on Tempahan — the Pakej itself acts as the category
5. Homepage: strictly hero + intro + CTAs + floating WhatsApp — what's in spec only

## Tech Stack
- Next.js (monolith), TypeScript, MySQL, Prisma, Tailwind CSS, NextAuth.js
- VPS hosting, single admin user
- Mobile-first for client pages, Bahasa Malaysia only

## Colour Palette
- Primary: #07203F (deep navy) — headers, nav, buttons
- Secondary: #EBDED4 (warm cream) — backgrounds, cards
- Accent 1: #D9AA90 (dusty rose) — CTAs, highlights
- Accent 2: #A65E46 (terracotta) — active states, badges
- Dark: #02000D (near-black) — body text

## Public Pages (Client-Side — HIGH RIGOR)
- Homepage `/` — hero, intro, CTAs to /temujanji and /kalendar, floating WhatsApp
- Temujanji `/temujanji` — show available 2-hour consultation slots, visitor books with Nama + No. Telefon + Nota
- Kalendar `/kalendar` — monthly calendar, shows booked events colour-coded by pakej, read-only
- Pakej `/pakej` — static showcase of 3 package types

## Admin Modules (STANDARD RIGOR — covered in second half)
- Slot Temujanji, Temujanji, Slot Tempahan, Tempahan, Pakej, Pelanggan

## Data Model
- User (single admin)
- Pelanggan (nama, noTelefon, nota)
- SlotTemujanji (tarikh, masaMula, masaTamat, status) — 2-hour ranges
- Temujanji (pelangganId, slotTemujanjiId, nota)
- SlotTempahan (tarikh, status)
- Tempahan (namaTempahan, slotTempahanId, pakejId, pelangganId) — NO kategori
- Pakej (namaPakej, warna/colour for calendar, gambar)
- PakejTier (pakejId, bilanganPax, harga)
- PakejSection (pakejId, tajuk, items)

## Task Format Required
Each task must include:
- **ID** — hierarchical (e.g., 1.1, 2.3.1)
- **Title** — imperative and specific
- **Description** — 1-3 sentences
- **Acceptance criteria** — testable checklist
- **Technical notes** — implementation hints
- **Dependencies** — other task IDs
- **Estimate** — t-shirt size (XS/S/M/L/XL)
- **Out of scope** — what this task does NOT include

## CLIENT-SIDE tasks need FULL treatment:
- States: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, empty, success
- Responsive: mobile / tablet / desktop
- Accessibility: keyboard nav, screen reader, focus management, contrast, reduced motion
- Data: where state lives, fetching strategy, loading/error handling
- Edge cases: empty data, long text, slow network
- Performance: memoization, lazy-loading, re-render risks
- Polish: transitions, micro-interactions, skeleton loaders

## File Header
Start with:
```
# MawarBiru — Task List

## Overview
MawarBiru is a luxury wedding and event venue website...
(include a note about client vs admin rigor split)

## Dependency Overview
(brief dependency chain between phases)
```

Now write phases 1-4. Be thorough on client-side tasks, concise but complete.

Write the SECOND HALF of a tasks.md file for the MawarBiru project. I'll give you the full context below. Write ONLY phases 5-8 (Cross-cutting Concerns, Admin Surface, Quality & Polish, Release Prep), plus Assumptions and Open Questions sections at the bottom.

## Project Summary
MawarBiru is a wedding/event venue website. Public visitors browse packages, book consultation appointments (temujanji), check venue availability via calendar. Business owner manages everything through admin panel. Next.js monolith with MySQL + Prisma, luxury aesthetic navy-and-cream palette.

## Key Clarifications from Owner
1. Pakej page is STATIC — hardcoded content
2. Temujanji slots are TIME RANGES of 2 hours
3. Kalendar colour-coding is ADMIN-CONFIGURABLE per pakej (colour picker)
4. NO kategori on Tempahan — Pakej acts as category
5. Homepage: strictly what's in spec

## Tech Stack
- Next.js (monolith), TypeScript, MySQL, Prisma, Tailwind CSS, NextAuth.js
- VPS hosting, single admin user
- Mobile-first for client pages, Bahasa Malaysia only

## Colour Palette
- Primary: #07203F (deep navy), Secondary: #EBDED4 (warm cream), Accent 1: #D9AA90 (dusty rose), Accent 2: #A65E46 (terracotta), Dark: #02000D (near-black)

## Data Model
- User (single admin)
- Pelanggan (nama, noTelefon, nota)
- SlotTemujanji (tarikh, masaMula, masaTamat, status) — 2-hour ranges
- Temujanji (pelangganId, slotTemujanjiId, nota)
- SlotTempahan (tarikh, status)
- Tempahan (namaTempahan, slotTempahanId, pakejId, pelangganId) — NO kategori
- Pakej (namaPakej, warna/colour for calendar, gambar)
- PakejTier (pakejId, bilanganPax, harga)
- PakejSection (pakejId, tajuk, items)

## Admin Modules (STANDARD RIGOR)
These are internal tools. Functionality over finesse. Use conventional CRUD patterns.

### Pengurusan Slot Temujanji
- CRUD for 2-hour consultation slots
- Fields: Tarikh, Masa Mula, Masa Tamat, Status (Tersedia/Ditempah)

### Pengurusan Temujanji
- View all consultation bookings
- Shows: Pelanggan info, Slot details, Nota
- Admin can update status or delete

### Pengurusan Slot Tempahan
- CRUD for venue booking date slots
- Fields: Tarikh, Status (Tersedia/Ditempah)
- Calendar view for managing

### Pengurusan Tempahan
- Create booking: click available slot → creation form
- Fields: Nama Tempahan, Pakej (select), Pelanggan (select) — NO kategori
- View, edit, delete bookings
- Booked events appear on public /kalendar

### Pengurusan Pakej
- CRUD for packages
- Fields: Nama Pakej, Warna (colour picker for calendar), Gambar (image upload)
- Tiers: dynamic list of Bilangan Pax + Harga
- Sections: dynamic list of Tajuk + bullet items

### Pengurusan Pelanggan
- View all customers (auto-created from temujanji or manual)
- Fields: Nama, No. Telefon, Nota
- Edit and delete

## Task Format for ADMIN (STANDARD treatment)
Admin tasks only need:
- ID, Title, Description
- Acceptance criteria (functional only)
- Technical notes (just the pattern)
- Dependencies + Estimate
- Keep it flat — one task can cover entire resource CRUD

## Phase 5: Cross-cutting Concerns (client-focused)
Cover: auth guards for admin routes, error boundaries, loading states, empty states, toast notifications, form validation patterns (for temujanji form), WhatsApp floating button component

## Phase 6: Admin Surface
ONE consolidated phase. Group by resource. One task per resource covering list + detail + create/edit + delete.
Also include: Admin layout/shell (sidebar nav, auth), Admin dashboard (simple overview)

## Phase 7: Quality & Polish (client-focused)
- Accessibility audit for client pages
- Responsive QA across breakpoints
- Performance (bundle size, lazy loading, image optimization, font loading)
- SEO (meta tags, SSR verification)
- Admin gets single "basic QA pass" task

## Phase 8: Release Prep
- Environment config (.env, secrets)
- Build pipeline
- VPS deployment (PM2/Docker)
- Domain + SSL setup
- Monitoring basics

## Bottom Sections

### Assumptions
List all assumptions made, especially:
- Slot temujanji = 2-hour ranges
- No kategori on tempahan
- Pakej colour = calendar colour coding
- Static pakej page
- Single admin, no roles
- etc.

### Open Questions
Remaining questions from spec + any new ones from task generation

### Summary Stats
At the very bottom, include:
- Total task count (client vs admin vs shared)
- Critical path
- 3 riskiest client-side tasks

```text
1	# MawarBiru — Spec
2	
3	## TL;DR
4	
5	MawarBiru is a wedding and event venue website for a small business owner. Public visitors (primarily from TikTok) can browse the venue's offerings, book consultation appointments (temujanji), and check venue availability via an event calendar. The business owner manages everything — slots, bookings, packages, and customers — through a protected admin panel. Built as a Next.js monolith with MySQL, styled in a luxury aesthetic with a navy-and-cream palette.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Problem & Users
10	
11	**Problem:** The business owner promotes his venue on TikTok but has no website for interested visitors to learn more, check availability, or book consultations. All coordination happens manually via WhatsApp.
12	
13	**Users:**
14	
15	| User | Description |
16	|------|-------------|
17	| **Pelawat (Visitor)** | Arrives from TikTok. Browses packages, checks venue availability, books a consultation. No account required. |
18	| **Admin (Business Owner)** | Single user. Manages all content, slots, bookings, and customer data via admin panel. |
19	
20	**One-sentence pitch:** A luxury venue showcase site with consultation booking and an admin CMS for the business owner.
21	
22	---
23	
24	## 2. Core User Journeys
25	
26	### Journey 1: Visitor Books a Consultation (Temujanji)
27	
28	1. Visitor sees TikTok video with link to `mawarbiru.my/temujanji`
29	2. Visitor lands on `/temujanji` page
30	3. Visitor sees available consultation slots (created by admin)
31	4. Visitor selects a slot
32	5. Visitor fills in: **Nama**, **No. Telefon**, **Nota**
33	6. Visitor submits — booking confirmed
34	7. A **Pelanggan** record is automatically created from the form data
35	
36	### Journey 2: Visitor Checks Venue Availability
37	
38	1. Visitor navigates to `/kalendar`
39	2. Visitor sees a monthly calendar view showing all booked events (colour-coded by category)
40	3. Visitor can navigate between months
41	4. **Read-only** — no actions available, just viewing
42	
43	### Journey 3: Admin Creates a Tempahan (Booking)
44	
45	1. Admin logs in to admin panel
46	2. Navigates to **Pengurusan Tempahan**
47	3. Views calendar of available slots (Slot Tempahan)
48	4. Clicks on an available date — redirected to tempahan creation form
49	5. Fills in: **Nama Tempahan**, selects **Pakej**, selects **Pelanggan**
50	6. Submits — tempahan created, slot marked as booked
51	7. Event appears on public `/kalendar`
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 3. Scope — MVP vs Later
56	
57	### In (MVP)
58	
59	- Single admin login (email + password)
60	- Admin modules: Slot Tempahan, Slot Temujanji, Tempahan, Temujanji, Pakej, Pelanggan
61	- Public pages: Homepage (`/`), Temujanji (`/temujanji`), Kalendar (`/kalendar`), Pakej (`/pakej` — static)
62	- Floating WhatsApp button on all public pages
63	- Mobile-first responsive design for customer-facing pages
64	- Luxury design with brand colour palette
65	- Bahasa Malaysia only
66	
67	### Out (Later)
68	
69	- Customer accounts / login
70	- Online payment
71	- Email / SMS notifications
72	- Analytics integration
73	- Multi-user admin / staff roles
74	- English language toggle
75	- List view for kalendar
76	- Dynamic pakej page (pulled from admin data)
77	
78	---
79	
80	## 4. Functional Requirements
81	
82	### 4.1 Public Pages
83	
84	#### Homepage (`/`)
85	
86	- Hero section with venue branding ("MawarBiru" text logo)
87	- Introduction to the business
88	- CTA buttons to `/temujanji` and `/kalendar`
89	- Floating WhatsApp button
90	
91	#### Temujanji (`/temujanji`)
92	
93	- Displays available consultation slots (from admin-managed Slot Temujanji)
94	- Visitor selects a slot and fills in:
95	  - Nama (required)
96	  - No. Telefon (required)
97	  - Nota (optional)
98	- On submit: creates Temujanji record + auto-creates Pelanggan record
99	- Booked slots no longer appear as available
100	
101	#### Kalendar (`/kalendar`)
102	
103	- Monthly calendar view
104	- Shows all Tempahan entries with:
105	  - Event name
106	  - Category (colour-coded: kahwin, aqiqah, open house, etc.)
107	  - Date
108	- Month navigation (previous/next)
109	- Read-only — no user interaction beyond navigation
110	
111	#### Pakej (`/pakej`)
112	
113	- Static page — content is hardcoded, not pulled from admin
114	- Showcases wedding and event packages
115	- Designed to match the luxury aesthetic
116	
117	### 4.2 Admin Modules
118	
119	All admin routes are protected behind authentication.
120	
121	#### Pengurusan Slot Temujanji
122	
123	- CRUD for consultation time slots
124	- Fields: Tarikh, Masa, Status (Tersedia / Ditempah)
125	- Admin can create, edit, delete slots
126	
127	#### Pengurusan Temujanji
128	
129	- View all consultation bookings
130	- Each record shows: Pelanggan info, Slot details, Nota
131	- Admin can update status or delete
132	
133	#### Pengurusan Slot Tempahan
134	
135	- CRUD for venue booking date slots
136	- Fields: Tarikh, Status (Tersedia / Ditempah)
137	- Calendar view for managing slots
138	
139	#### Pengurusan Tempahan
140	
141	- Create booking from calendar: click available slot → redirected to creation form
142	- Creation form fields:
143	  - Nama Tempahan (required)
144	  - Pakej (select from existing packages)
145	  - Pelanggan (select from existing customers)
146	  - Kategori: Kahwin, Aqiqah, Open House, etc.
147	- View, edit, delete existing bookings
148	- Booked events appear on public `/kalendar`
149	
150	#### Pengurusan Pakej
151	
152	- CRUD for packages
153	- Fields:
154	  - Nama Pakej (required)
155	  - Kategori (Perkahwinan, Acara, etc.)
156	  - Gambar (image upload)
157	  - Tiers — dynamic list of: Bilangan Pax + Harga
158	  - Sections — dynamic list of: Tajuk Section + bullet list of items
159	- Pakej data is used when creating Tempahan (admin-only, not public-facing)
160	
161	#### Pengurusan Pelanggan
162	
163	- View all customers
164	- Created automatically when visitor books temujanji, or manually by admin
165	- Fields: Nama, No. Telefon, Nota
166	- Edit and delete
167	
168	---
169	
170	## 5. Data Model
171	
172	### Entities
173	
174	| Entity | Fields | Notes |
175	|--------|--------|-------|
176	| **User** | id, email, password, createdAt, updatedAt | Single admin user |
177	| **Pelanggan** | id, nama, noTelefon, nota, createdAt, updatedAt | Auto-created from temujanji or manually |
178	| **SlotTemujanji** | id, tarikh, masa, status, createdAt, updatedAt | Status: TERSEDIA, DITEMPAH |
179	| **Temujanji** | id, pelangganId, slotTemujanjiId, nota, createdAt, updatedAt | Links to Pelanggan and SlotTemujanji |
180	| **SlotTempahan** | id, tarikh, status, createdAt, updatedAt | Status: TERSEDIA, DITEMPAH |
181	| **Tempahan** | id, namaTempahan, slotTempahanId, pakejId, pelangganId, kategori, createdAt, updatedAt | Kategori: KAHWIN, AQIQAH, OPEN_HOUSE, etc. |
182	| **Pakej** | id, namaPakej, kategori, gambar, createdAt, updatedAt | Parent entity for tiers and sections |
183	| **PakejTier** | id, pakejId, bilanganPax, harga | e.g., 300 PAX → RM15,500 |
184	| **PakejSection** | id, pakejId, tajuk, items (JSON or related table) | e.g., "Menu Kenduri" → list of food items |
185	
186	### Relationships
187	
188	```
189	User (standalone — single admin)
190	
191	Pelanggan 1 ← N Temujanji
192	SlotTemujanji 1 ← 1 Temujanji
193	
194	Pelanggan 1 ← N Tempahan
195	SlotTempahan 1 ← 1 Tempahan
196	Pakej 1 ← N Tempahan
197	
198	Pakej 1 ← N PakejTier
199	Pakej 1 ← N PakejSection
200	```
201	
202	### Data Privacy
203	
204	- No sensitive data requiring encryption beyond admin password (hashed)
205	- Customer data (nama, phone) is basic contact info
206	- No payment data stored
207	
208	---
209	
210	## 6. Auth & Permissions
211	
212	| Aspect | Decision |
213	|--------|----------|
214	| Auth method | Email + password |
215	| Users | Single admin account |
216	| Customer login | None — visitors are anonymous |
217	| Session | Server-side session or JWT |
218	| Roles | No role system — single admin only |
219	| Multi-tenant | No |
220	
221	---
222	
223	## 7. Tech Stack
224	
225	| Layer | Choice | Rationale |
226	|-------|--------|-----------|
227	| Framework | **Next.js** (monolith) | Full-stack, SSR for public pages, API routes for admin |
228	| Language | **TypeScript** | Type safety |
229	| Database | **MySQL** | Familiar, reliable |
230	| ORM | **Prisma** | Best DX with Next.js, type-safe queries |
231	| Styling | **Tailwind CSS** | Rapid UI development, mobile-first utilities |
232	| Hosting | **VPS** | Full control, cost-effective |
233	| Auth | **NextAuth.js** (or similar) | Simple credentials provider for single admin |
234	
235	---
236	
237	## 8. Integrations
238	
239	| Integration | Status |
240	|-------------|--------|
241	| WhatsApp | Floating button linking to `wa.me/<number>` |
242	| Payment gateway | Not in MVP |
243	| Email / SMS | Not in MVP |
244	| Analytics | Not in MVP |
245	
246	---
247	
248	## 9. Non-Functional Requirements
249	
250	| Aspect | Requirement |
251	|--------|-------------|
252	| Responsive | Mobile-first for all customer-facing pages |
253	| Language | Bahasa Malaysia only |
254	| Browser support | Modern browsers (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge) |
255	| Performance | Fast initial load — critical for TikTok traffic with short attention spans |
256	| Accessibility | Basic — semantic HTML, readable contrast |
257	| SEO | Basic meta tags, SSR for public pages |
258	
259	---
260	
261	## 10. Design
262	
263	### Branding
264	
265	- **Logo:** Text-based "MawarBiru" (no graphic logo yet)
266	- **Style:** Luxury / mewah
267	
268	### Colour Palette
269	
270	| Role | Hex | Description | Usage |
271	|------|-----|-------------|-------|
272	| Primary | `#07203F` | Deep navy | Headers, nav, buttons, key UI elements |
273	| Secondary | `#EBDED4` | Warm cream | Page backgrounds, cards |
274	| Accent 1 | `#D9AA90` | Dusty rose / peach | CTAs, highlights, hover states |
275	| Accent 2 | `#A65E46` | Terracotta | Active states, badges, small accents |
276	| Dark | `#02000D` | Near-black | Body text, footer |
277	
278	### Design Decisions
279	
280	- Navy + cream base for luxury feel with high contrast
281	- Dusty rose for CTAs — draws attention without being loud
282	- Terracotta used sparingly for warmth
283	- Near-black for text instead of pure black — softer on eyes
284	
285	---
286	
287	## 11. Constraints
288	
289	| Constraint | Detail |
290	|------------|--------|
291	| Budget | Minimal — self-hosted VPS |
292	| Team | Solo developer (Claude-assisted) |
293	| Timeline | Not specified |
294	| Domain | `mawarbiru.my` (TBC if secured) |
295	
296	---
297	
298	## 12. Open Questions
299	
300	1. **Domain** — Is `mawarbiru.my` already registered?
301	2. **VPS** — Is there an existing VPS, or does one need to be provisioned?
302	3. **WhatsApp number** — Which phone number(s) for the floating button?
303	4. **Content** — Are photos and text ready for the static homepage and pakej page?
304	5. **Tempahan categories** — Is the list (Kahwin, Aqiqah, Open House) final, or should admin be able to add custom categories?
305	6. **Slot Temujanji structure** — Is it one slot per time (e.g., 10:00 AM on 1 May), or a range (e.g., 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM)?
306	7. **Calendar colour-coding** — Should each tempahan kategori have a fixed colour, or admin-configurable?
307
```
</details></tool-use>

18	
19	@spec.md read this and generate a tasks for this project in @claude/resources/tasks/ folder
20	
21	You are a senior frontend engineer with 10+ years of production experience 
22	shipping web applications. I have a `spec.md` file describing a web app. 
23	Your job is to read it carefully and break it down into a detailed, 
24	executable task list — written to `tasks.md`.
25	
26	## Scope priority (READ THIS FIRST)
27	
28	This app has two surfaces: a **client-facing side** (end users) and an 
29	**admin side** (internal operators). Your effort should be split 
30	accordingly:
31	
32	- **Client-side frontend: HIGH RIGOR.** This is what users see, judge, 
33	  and abandon us over. Every UI task gets the full treatment — states, 
34	  responsive, accessibility, edge cases, performance, micro-interactions, 
35	  visual polish.
36	- **Admin-side frontend: STANDARD RIGOR.** Internal tool. Functionality 
37	  over finesse. Use conventional CRUD patterns, off-the-shelf table/form 
38	  components, and sensible defaults. Do NOT write detailed per-state 
39	  breakdowns for admin screens. A task like "Build users admin table 
40	  with search, pagination, row actions (edit/delete), and a modal edit 
41	  form" is enough — don't expand it into 15 sub-tasks.
42	- **Backend, infra, DB:** lightweight, included for completeness only.
43	
44	If the spec doesn't clearly label something as client vs admin, ask me 
45	before generating tasks.
46	
47	## Before you start
48	
49	1. Read `spec.md` in full before writing anything.
50	2. If the spec is ambiguous, incomplete, or contradicts itself on anything 
51	   that affects **client-side** implementation (routing, state, data shape, 
52	   auth flow, responsive behavior, accessibility expectations), STOP and 
53	   ask me clarifying questions. Ambiguity on admin-side details can be 
54	   resolved with reasonable defaults and noted in Assumptions.
55	
56	## How to structure the task list
57	
58	Organize `tasks.md` into these top-level phases, in order:
59	
60	1. **Foundation** — repo setup, tooling, linting, formatting, CI basics, 
61	   folder structure, design tokens, base theming
62	2. **Design system & primitives** — reusable components used by the 
63	   CLIENT side (Button, Input, Modal, Card, etc.), typography scale, 
64	   spacing scale, color tokens. Admin can reuse these but doesn't drive 
65	   the design.
66	3. **Client layout & navigation** — app shell, routing, nav, responsive 
67	   breakpoints for the public/user-facing side
68	4. **Client feature slices** — one phase per major client-facing feature 
69	   from the spec, each broken down end-to-end (UI → state → data → edge 
70	   cases → polish)
71	5. **Cross-cutting concerns (client)** — auth guards, error boundaries, 
72	   loading states, empty states, toasts, form validation patterns
73	6. **Admin surface** — ONE consolidated phase for the entire admin side. 
74	   Group by resource (Users, Orders, etc.), one task per resource covering 
75	   list + detail + create/edit + delete. Keep it flat.
76	7. **Quality & polish (client-focused)** — accessibility audit, responsive 
77	   QA, performance (bundle size, lazy loading, image optimization), 
78	   analytics. Admin gets a single "basic QA pass" task.
79	8. **Release prep** — env config, build pipeline, deployment, monitoring
80	
81	## How to write each task
82	
83	Each task must include:
84	
85	- **ID** — hierarchical (e.g., `4.3.1`)
86	- **Title** — imperative and specific
87	- **Description** — 1-3 sentences of what and why
88	- **Acceptance criteria** — testable checklist
89	- **Technical notes** — implementation hints, patterns, pitfalls
90	- **Dependencies** — other task IDs that must land first
91	- **Estimate** — t-shirt size (XS / S / M / L / XL)
92	- **Out of scope** — what this task deliberately does NOT include
93	
94	## Rigor rules by surface
95	
96	### Client-side UI tasks — FULL treatment
97	
98	Every client-facing UI task must address:
99	
100	- **States**: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, 
101	  empty, success. If a state doesn't apply, say so explicitly.
102	- **Responsive**: behavior at mobile / tablet / desktop
103	- **Accessibility**: keyboard nav, screen reader labels, focus management, 
104	  color contrast, reduced motion
105	- **Data**: where state lives (local / URL / server / global), fetching 
106	  strategy, loading/error handling, optimistic updates
107	- **Edge cases**: empty data, long text, slow network, offline, permission 
108	  denied, rate limits
109	- **Performance**: memoization, lazy-loading, image/font strategy, 
110	  re-render risks
111	- **Polish**: transitions, micro-interactions, skeleton loaders where 
112	  appropriate
113	
114	Client tasks should be sized small. Prefer "build `<LoginForm>` with 
115	email + password, client-side validation, submit handler calling 
116	`POST /auth/login`, error display for 401/422/network, loading spinner, 
117	redirect on success" over "build login page."
118	
119	### Admin-side UI tasks — STANDARD treatment
120	
121	Admin tasks only need:
122	
123	- **Title + description**
124	- **Acceptance criteria** (functional only — "admin can create, edit, 
125	  delete users; list is searchable and paginated")
126	- **Technical notes** (just the pattern — "use the shared DataTable 
127	  primitive and FormDialog; no custom work needed")
128	- **Dependencies + estimate**
129	
130	Skip the states checklist, skip the responsive breakdown (assume desktop-
131	first, usable on tablet, mobile not required unless spec says otherwise), 
132	skip per-micro-interaction polish. Assume admins are tolerant users on 
133	modern browsers.
134	
135	Admin tasks should be sized larger — one task can legitimately cover an 
136	entire resource's CRUD screens.
137	
138	## Output format
139	
140	- Write to `tasks.md` as structured markdown
141	- H2 for phases, H3 for feature groups, consistent task block format
142	- Top of file: one-paragraph app summary + dependency overview + note on 
143	  the client/admin rigor split so future readers understand the density 
144	  difference
145	- Bottom: "Assumptions" section and "Open questions" section
146	- After writing, summarize: total task count (broken down client vs 
147	  admin vs shared), critical path, and the 3 riskiest client-side tasks
148	
149	## Definition of done for you
150	
151	- Any client-side task should be executable by a junior-to-mid engineer 
152	  without re-reading the spec or asking questions.
153	- Admin tasks can assume a competent engineer who knows CRUD patterns 
154	  will fill in obvious details.
155	- The density difference between client and admin sections in `tasks.md` 
156	  should be visually obvious when skimmed.
157	
158	Start by reading `spec.md`. If you have questions about client-side 
159	behavior or about which features are client vs admin, ask them now. 
160	Otherwise, generate `tasks.md`.
161	
162	---

319	## Project Summary
320	MawarBiru is a wedding/event venue website. Public visitors browse packages, book consultation appointments (temujanji), check venue availability via calendar. Business owner manages everything through admin panel. Next.js monolith with MySQL + Prisma, luxury aesthetic navy-and-cream palette.
321	
322	## Key Clarifications from Owner
323	1. Pakej page is STATIC — hardcoded content based on 3 packages: Night Wedding (300/500/800 PAX), Pakej wedding (Seroja 500/Melur 800/Teratai 1000 PAX), Event Package (Breakfast/Lunch/Hi-Tea/Dinner per-pax + Hall Rental)
324	2. Temujanji slots are TIME RANGES of 2 hours (e.g., "10:00 - 12:00")
325	3. Kalendar colour-coding is ADMIN-CONFIGURABLE per pakej (colour picker in admin)
326	4. NO kategori on Tempahan — the Pakej itself acts as the category
327	5. Homepage: strictly hero + intro + CTAs + floating WhatsApp — what's in spec only
328	
329	## Tech Stack
330	- Next.js (monolith), TypeScript, MySQL, Prisma, Tailwind CSS, NextAuth.js
331	- VPS hosting, single admin user
332	- Mobile-first for client pages, Bahasa Malaysia only
333	
334	## Colour Palette
335	- Primary: #07203F (deep navy) — headers, nav, buttons
336	- Secondary: #EBDED4 (warm cream) — backgrounds, cards
337	- Accent 1: #D9AA90 (dusty rose) — CTAs, highlights
338	- Accent 2: #A65E46 (terracotta) — active states, badges
339	- Dark: #02000D (near-black) — body text
340	
341	## Public Pages (Client-Side — HIGH RIGOR)
342	- Homepage `/` — hero, intro, CTAs to /temujanji and /kalendar, floating WhatsApp
343	- Temujanji `/temujanji` — show available 2-hour consultation slots, visitor books with Nama + No. Telefon + Nota
344	- Kalendar `/kalendar` — monthly calendar, shows booked events colour-coded by pakej, read-only
345	- Pakej `/pakej` — static showcase of 3 package types
346	
347	## Admin Modules (STANDARD RIGOR — covered in second half)
348	- Slot Temujanji, Temujanji, Slot Tempahan, Tempahan, Pakej, Pelanggan
349	
350	## Data Model
351	- User (single admin)
352	- Pelanggan (nama, noTelefon, nota)
353	- SlotTemujanji (tarikh, masaMula, masaTamat, status) — 2-hour ranges
354	- Temujanji (pelangganId, slotTemujanjiId, nota)
355	- SlotTempahan (tarikh, status)
356	- Tempahan (namaTempahan, slotTempahanId, pakejId, pelangganId) — NO kategori
357	- Pakej (namaPakej, warna/colour for calendar, gambar)
358	- PakejTier (pakejId, bilanganPax, harga)
359	- PakejSection (pakejId, tajuk, items)
360	
361	## Task Format Required
362	Each task must include:
363	- **ID** — hierarchical (e.g., 1.1, 2.3.1)
364	- **Title** — imperative and specific
365	- **Description** — 1-3 sentences
366	- **Acceptance criteria** — testable checklist
367	- **Technical notes** — implementation hints
368	- **Dependencies** — other task IDs
369	- **Estimate** — t-shirt size (XS/S/M/L/XL)
370	- **Out of scope** — what this task does NOT include
371	
372	## CLIENT-SIDE tasks need FULL treatment:
373	- States: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, empty, success
374	- Responsive: mobile / tablet / desktop
375	- Accessibility: keyboard nav, screen reader, focus management, contrast, reduced motion
376	- Data: where state lives, fetching strategy, loading/error handling
377	- Edge cases: empty data, long text, slow network
378	- Performance: memoization, lazy-loading, re-render risks
379	- Polish: transitions, micro-interactions, skeleton loaders
380

415	
416	## Project Summary
417	MawarBiru is a wedding/event venue website. Public visitors browse packages, book consultation appointments (temujanji), check venue availability via calendar. Business owner manages everything through admin panel. Next.js monolith with MySQL + Prisma, luxury aesthetic navy-and-cream palette.
418	
419	## Key Clarifications from Owner
420	1. Pakej page is STATIC — hardcoded content
421	2. Temujanji slots are TIME RANGES of 2 hours
422	3. Kalendar colour-coding is ADMIN-CONFIGURABLE per pakej (colour picker)
423	4. NO kategori on Tempahan — Pakej acts as category
424	5. Homepage: strictly what's in spec
425	
426	## Tech Stack
427	- Next.js (monolith), TypeScript, MySQL, Prisma, Tailwind CSS, NextAuth.js
428	- VPS hosting, single admin user
429	- Mobile-first for client pages, Bahasa Malaysia only
430	
431	## Colour Palette
432	- Primary: #07203F (deep navy), Secondary: #EBDED4 (warm cream), Accent 1: #D9AA90 (dusty rose), Accent 2: #A65E46 (terracotta), Dark: #02000D (near-black)
433	
434	## Data Model
435	- User (single admin)
436	- Pelanggan (nama, noTelefon, nota)
437	- SlotTemujanji (tarikh, masaMula, masaTamat, status) — 2-hour ranges
438	- Temujanji (pelangganId, slotTemujanjiId, nota)
439	- SlotTempahan (tarikh, status)
440	- Tempahan (namaTempahan, slotTempahanId, pakejId, pelangganId) — NO kategori
441	- Pakej (namaPakej, warna/colour for calendar, gambar)
442	- PakejTier (pakejId, bilanganPax, harga)
443	- PakejSection (pakejId, tajuk, items)
444	
445	## Admin Modules (STANDARD RIGOR)
446	These are internal tools. Functionality over finesse. Use conventional CRUD patterns.
447	
448	### Pengurusan Slot Temujanji
449	- CRUD for 2-hour consultation slots
450	- Fields: Tarikh, Masa Mula, Masa Tamat, Status (Tersedia/Ditempah)
451	
452	### Pengurusan Temujanji
453	- View all consultation bookings
454	- Shows: Pelanggan info, Slot details, Nota
455	- Admin can update status or delete
456	
457	### Pengurusan Slot Tempahan
458	- CRUD for venue booking date slots
459	- Fields: Tarikh, Status (Tersedia/Ditempah)
460	- Calendar view for managing
461	
462	### Pengurusan Tempahan
463	- Create booking: click available slot → creation form
464	- Fields: Nama Tempahan, Pakej (select), Pelanggan (select) — NO kategori
465	- View, edit, delete bookings
466	- Booked events appear on public /kalendar
467	
468	### Pengurusan Pakej
469	- CRUD for packages
470	- Fields: Nama Pakej, Warna (colour picker for calendar), Gambar (image upload)
471	- Tiers: dynamic list of Bilangan Pax + Harga
472	- Sections: dynamic list of Tajuk + bullet items
473	
474	### Pengurusan Pelanggan
475	- View all customers (auto-created from temujanji or manual)
476	- Fields: Nama, No. Telefon, Nota
477	- Edit and delete
478	
479	## Task Format for ADMIN (STANDARD treatment)
480	Admin tasks only need:
481	- ID, Title, Description
482	- Acceptance criteria (functional only)
483	- Technical notes (just the pattern)
484	- Dependencies + Estimate
485	- Keep it flat — one task can cover entire resource CRUD
486	
487	## Phase 5: Cross-cutting Concerns (client-focused)
488	Cover: auth guards for admin routes, error boundaries, loading states, empty states, toast notifications, form validation patterns (for temujanji form), WhatsApp floating button component
489	
490	## Phase 6: Admin Surface
491	ONE consolidated phase. Group by resource. One task per resource covering list + detail + create/edit + delete.
492	Also include: Admin layout/shell (sidebar nav, auth), Admin dashboard (simple overview)
493	
494	## Phase 7: Quality & Polish (client-focused)
495	- Accessibility audit for client pages
496	- Responsive QA across breakpoints
497	- Performance (bundle size, lazy loading, image optimization, font loading)
498	- SEO (meta tags, SSR verification)
499	- Admin gets single "basic QA pass" task
500	
501	## Phase 8: Release Prep
502	- Environment config (.env, secrets)
503	- Build pipeline
504	- VPS deployment (PM2/Docker)
505	- Domain + SSL setup
506	- Monitoring basics
507	
508	## Bottom Sections
509	
510	### Assumptions
511	List all assumptions made, especially:
512	- Slot temujanji = 2-hour ranges
513	- No kategori on tempahan
514	- Pakej colour = calendar colour coding
515	- Static pakej page
516	- Single admin, no roles
517	- etc.
518	
519	### Open Questions
520	Remaining questions from spec + any new ones from task generation
521	
522	### Summary Stats
523	At the very bottom, include:
524	- Total task count (client vs admin vs shared)
525	- Critical path
526	- 3 riskiest client-side tasks
527

9	You are a senior product engineer helping me define the specification for a 
10	web application. Your job is to interview me through a structured discovery 
11	process, then write the final spec to `spec.md`.
12	
13	## Rules of engagement
14	
15	1. Ask me ONE question at a time (or a small tight cluster of related 
16	   sub-questions). Do NOT dump a long questionnaire on me at once.
17	2. Wait for my answer before moving to the next question.
18	3. If my answer is vague, ambiguous, or contradicts something I said earlier, 
19	   push back and ask for clarification. Do not accept hand-wavy answers.
20	4. If I say "I don't know" or "you decide," propose 2-3 concrete options with 
21	   tradeoffs and let me pick.
22	5. Track what we've covered. After each of my answers, briefly acknowledge 
23	   what you've captured before moving on.
24	6. Do NOT write the spec until I explicitly say "write the spec" or you have 
25	   covered every section below and confirmed with me.
26	
27	## Topics to cover (in roughly this order)
28	
29	1. **Problem & users** — What problem does this solve? Who is the user? What 
30	   is the one-sentence pitch?
31	2. **Core user journeys** — Walk through the 2-3 most important flows from 
32	   the user's perspective, step by step.
33	3. **Scope — MVP vs later** — What is explicitly IN the first version? What 
34	   is explicitly OUT (but might come later)?
35	4. **Functional requirements** — Features, broken down. For each: what does 
36	   it do, who can access it, what are the edge cases?
37	5. **Data model** — What are the core entities, their fields, and how do they 
38	   relate? Any data that must be kept private or encrypted?
39	6. **Auth & permissions** — Anonymous? Logged-in? Roles? Social login? 
40	   Multi-tenant?
41	7. **Tech stack preferences** — Frontend framework, backend, database, 
42	   hosting. If I have no preference, recommend based on the project's shape.
43	8. **Integrations** — Third-party APIs, payment, email, analytics, etc.
44	9. **Non-functional requirements** — Expected load, performance targets, 
45	   browser/device support, accessibility, i18n.
46	10. **Constraints** — Budget, timeline, team size, my skill level with the 
47	    chosen stack.
48	11. **Success criteria** — How will we know the MVP is working? What metric 
49	    or behavior defines "it shipped"?
50	12. **Open questions & risks** — Things I'm unsure about or that could kill 
51	    the project.
52	
53	## When you write `spec.md`
54	
55	- Use clear markdown with H2 sections matching the topics above.
56	- Start with a TL;DR: one paragraph summarizing the app.
57	- Use tables for data models and permissions matrices.
58	- Include a "Decisions & rationale" subsection anywhere I made a non-obvious 
59	  choice, capturing WHY not just WHAT.
60	- End with an "Open questions" section for anything unresolved.
61	- After writing the file, give me a short summary of what's in it and ask if 
62	  I want to revise any section.
63	
64	Start by asking me your first question.

# Phase 5: Cross-cutting Concerns (Client-focused)

These are shared infrastructure components that multiple client-facing pages depend on. They enforce consistency across the public surface and reduce duplication.

---

## 5.1 Admin Route Auth Guard Middleware

**Description:** Create a Next.js middleware that protects all `/admin/*` routes. Unauthenticated requests redirect to a login page. Uses NextAuth.js session check.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] All routes under `/admin` require valid session
- [ ] Unauthenticated users are redirected to `/admin/login`
- [ ] Session check happens server-side via NextAuth middleware
- [ ] Authenticated users proceed normally
- [ ] Login page is excluded from the guard (no redirect loop)
- [ ] API routes under `/api/admin/*` return 401 JSON for unauthenticated requests

**Technical notes:**
- Use Next.js `middleware.ts` with `matcher` config targeting `/admin/:path*`
- Check session via `getToken()` from `next-auth/jwt`
- Keep middleware lightweight; no DB calls, JWT check only
- API routes should return `{ error: "Tidak dibenarkan" }` with 401 status

**Dependencies:** 1.x (Foundation — NextAuth setup)
**Estimate:** S

---

## 5.2 Global Error Boundary for Client Pages

**Description:** Wrap all public pages in an error boundary that catches rendering errors and displays a branded fallback UI instead of a white screen. Server errors during SSR should also render a styled error page.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Runtime errors in client pages show a branded error screen
- [ ] Error screen matches MawarBiru luxury aesthetic (navy/cream palette)
- [ ] Error screen includes: apologetic message in Bahasa Malaysia, "Kembali ke Laman Utama" CTA
- [ ] `error.tsx` file exists at the app route level
- [ ] `global-error.tsx` exists for root layout failures
- [ ] Errors are logged to console (monitoring hook point for later)
- [ ] Error boundary does not affect admin pages (admin has own error handling)

**States:**
- Default: Normal rendering, boundary invisible
- Error: Branded fallback with message + CTA button
- Hover/Focus on CTA: Standard button states from design system

**Responsive:**
- Mobile: Centered layout, stacked content, full-width CTA
- Tablet/Desktop: Centered card with constrained max-width

**Accessibility:**
- Focus moves to the error heading when boundary activates
- CTA is keyboard accessible
- Screen reader announces error context

**Technical notes:**
- Use Next.js App Router `error.tsx` convention
- Include a `reset()` callback button to retry rendering
- Style with Tailwind using brand tokens from Phase 2
- Keep error page self-contained (no external data fetching)

**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system — Button, typography tokens)
**Estimate:** S
**Out of scope:** Error tracking/monitoring service integration (Phase 8)

---

## 5.3 Loading States and Skeleton Loaders

**Description:** Create reusable loading components: a full-page skeleton loader, inline skeleton primitives (text lines, cards, calendar grid), and a branded spinner. These are used across Temujanji, Kalendar, and layout transitions.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `<SkeletonText>` component renders animated placeholder lines (configurable count)
- [ ] `<SkeletonCard>` renders a card-shaped placeholder matching the slot card design
- [ ] `<SkeletonCalendar>` renders a calendar-grid-shaped placeholder for `/kalendar`
- [ ] `<LoadingSpinner>` renders a branded spinner using MawarBiru palette
- [ ] `loading.tsx` files exist for `/temujanji` and `/kalendar` routes
- [ ] Skeleton pulse animation uses `#EBDED4` (cream) to `#D9AA90` (dusty rose) gradient
- [ ] Animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion` — falls back to static placeholder

**States:**
- Loading: Animated pulse/shimmer
- Reduced motion: Static grey placeholders, no animation

**Responsive:**
- Skeletons match the responsive layout of the content they replace
- Mobile: Single-column skeletons
- Desktop: Multi-column where the real content is multi-column

**Accessibility:**
- Skeleton containers have `aria-busy="true"` and `role="status"`
- Screen reader: `aria-label="Memuatkan..."` on the container

**Technical notes:**
- Use Tailwind `animate-pulse` as base, customize with brand colours
- Export from a shared `components/ui/skeleton/` directory
- `loading.tsx` files leverage Next.js App Router Suspense integration automatically

**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system tokens)
**Estimate:** M
**Out of scope:** Admin-side loading states (handled within admin components)

---

## 5.4 Empty State Components

**Description:** Create branded empty state displays for when data-driven pages have no content. Primary consumers: Temujanji page (no available slots), Kalendar (no events in selected month).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `<EmptyState>` component accepts: icon (optional), title, description, CTA (optional)
- [ ] Temujanji empty state: "Tiada slot tersedia" with suggestion to check back later or contact via WhatsApp
- [ ] Kalendar empty state: "Tiada acara pada bulan ini" with month navigation hint
- [ ] Styled with MawarBiru palette — subtle, not alarming
- [ ] Each empty state includes an optional WhatsApp CTA link

**States:**
- Default: Centered illustration/icon + text + optional CTA
- Hover/Focus on CTA: Standard button hover from design system

**Responsive:**
- Mobile: Full-width centered, comfortable padding
- Desktop: Constrained max-width, centered in content area

**Accessibility:**
- Empty state region has `role="status"` so screen readers announce it
- CTA is keyboard navigable

**Technical notes:**
- Generic `<EmptyState>` component; page-specific instances pass props
- Use a simple SVG icon or emoji placeholder (luxury-appropriate)
- Place in `components/ui/empty-state/`

**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system — typography, Button)
**Estimate:** S
**Out of scope:** Admin empty states (use basic "Tiada rekod" text)

---

## 5.5 Toast Notification System

**Description:** Implement a toast notification system for success/error feedback on the Temujanji booking form and admin actions. Toasts appear at the top or bottom of the viewport, auto-dismiss after a timeout, and are dismissible manually.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `<ToastProvider>` wraps the app and manages toast state
- [ ] `useToast()` hook exposes `showToast({ type, message, duration? })` function
- [ ] Toast types: `success` (green/dusty-rose), `error` (terracotta/red), `info` (navy)
- [ ] Toasts auto-dismiss after 5 seconds (configurable)
- [ ] Toasts can be manually dismissed with close button
- [ ] Multiple toasts stack vertically
- [ ] Toast entrance/exit has slide + fade animation
- [ ] Animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion`

**States:**
- Hidden: No toast visible
- Visible: Toast slides in with message
- Hover on toast: Pause auto-dismiss timer
- Dismissing: Slide + fade out

**Responsive:**
- Mobile: Full-width toast at bottom of viewport
- Desktop: Fixed-width toast at top-right corner

**Accessibility:**
- Toast container has `role="alert"` and `aria-live="polite"`
- Close button has `aria-label="Tutup"`
- Keyboard: close button focusable, Escape dismisses topmost toast

**Technical notes:**
- Use React Context + `useReducer` for toast state management
- Consider `sonner` or `react-hot-toast` as lightweight alternatives to custom implementation
- If using a library, ensure it supports custom styling with Tailwind
- Place provider in root layout

**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system tokens)
**Estimate:** M
**Out of scope:** Admin gets the same toast system (shared), but no additional admin-specific toast types

---

## 5.6 Temujanji Form Validation Pattern

**Description:** Implement client-side and server-side validation for the Temujanji booking form. This establishes the validation pattern used across the app.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Client-side validation using Zod schema
- [ ] Fields validated: Nama (required, min 2 chars, max 100), No. Telefon (required, Malaysian format), Nota (optional, max 500 chars)
- [ ] Malaysian phone validation: accepts `01x-xxxxxxx` patterns (with or without dashes, with or without `+60` prefix)
- [ ] Inline error messages appear below each field in Bahasa Malaysia
- [ ] Errors display on blur and on submit attempt
- [ ] Submit button is disabled during submission (loading state)
- [ ] Server-side validation mirrors client-side Zod schema (shared schema file)
- [ ] API returns structured error response: `{ errors: { field: string[] } }`

**States:**
- Default: Empty form, no errors shown
- Touched + invalid: Inline error below field, field border turns terracotta (`#A65E46`)
- Touched + valid: Field border returns to default (or subtle green check)
- Submitting: Button shows spinner, all fields disabled
- Success: Toast notification, form resets or redirects
- Server error: Toast with error message, form remains filled

**Responsive:**
- Mobile: Stacked fields, full-width inputs
- Tablet/Desktop: Same stacked layout (form doesn't need multi-column)

**Accessibility:**
- Error messages linked to fields via `aria-describedby`
- Invalid fields have `aria-invalid="true"`
- Focus moves to first error field on failed submit
- Error summary announced to screen readers

**Technical notes:**
- Create shared Zod schema in `lib/validations/temujanji.ts`
- Use `react-hook-form` with `@hookform/resolvers/zod` for client-side
- Server action or API route reuses same Zod schema
- Phone regex: `/^(\+?60)?0?1[0-9]-?[0-9]{7,8}$/`

**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system — Input, Button), 4.x (Temujanji feature slice — form structure)
**Estimate:** M
**Out of scope:** SMS/OTP verification, email validation

---

## 5.7 WhatsApp Floating Button Component

**Description:** Build a floating WhatsApp button that appears on all public pages. It links to the business owner's WhatsApp number via `wa.me` URL. Persistent across page navigations, visually prominent but not obstructing content.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Floating circular button fixed to bottom-right corner of viewport
- [ ] WhatsApp green icon (SVG) on the button
- [ ] Clicking opens `https://wa.me/<configured-number>` in new tab
- [ ] WhatsApp number is configurable via environment variable (`NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER`)
- [ ] Button has subtle entrance animation on page load (fade + slide up)
- [ ] Button has hover scale effect
- [ ] Button does not overlap page footer when scrolled to bottom (offset margin)
- [ ] Button is visible on all public pages (`/`, `/temujanji`, `/kalendar`, `/pakej`)
- [ ] Button is NOT visible on admin pages

**States:**
- Default: Green circular button with WhatsApp icon, slight shadow
- Hover: Scale up to 1.1x, shadow intensifies
- Focus: Visible focus ring (for keyboard users)
- Active: Scale down to 0.95x (press effect)

**Responsive:**
- Mobile: 48x48px minimum touch target, 16px from edges
- Tablet: 56x56px, 24px from edges
- Desktop: 56x56px, 32px from edges

**Accessibility:**
- `aria-label="Hubungi kami melalui WhatsApp"`
- `target="_blank"` with `rel="noopener noreferrer"`
- Keyboard focusable (naturally, as an `<a>` tag)
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` for entrance animation

**Technical notes:**
- Implement as a client component in `components/ui/whatsapp-button.tsx`
- Include in the public layout (not root layout, to exclude admin)
- Use inline SVG for the WhatsApp icon (avoid external dependency)
- `z-index` should be below modals but above page content (e.g., `z-40`)

**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system tokens)
**Estimate:** S
**Out of scope:** WhatsApp chat widget, pre-filled message templates

```

```md
# Phase 6: Admin Surface

All admin modules use STANDARD rigor. Functionality over finesse. Desktop-first, conventional CRUD patterns. One task per resource unless complexity warrants splitting.

---

## 6.1 Admin Layout Shell and Authentication

**Description:** Build the admin layout wrapper: sidebar navigation, top bar with logout, and the NextAuth.js credentials login page. This is the frame for all admin modules.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `/admin/login` page with email + password form
- [ ] NextAuth.js credentials provider authenticates against single admin User record
- [ ] Password stored as bcrypt hash in DB
- [ ] Successful login redirects to `/admin` dashboard
- [ ] Failed login shows inline error: "E-mel atau kata laluan salah"
- [ ] Admin layout has collapsible sidebar with navigation links to all modules
- [ ] Sidebar nav items: Dashboard, Slot Temujanji, Temujanji, Slot Tempahan, Tempahan, Pakej, Pelanggan
- [ ] Top bar shows admin name/email and logout button
- [ ] Logout clears session and redirects to `/admin/login`
- [ ] Sidebar collapses to icons on narrow viewports (tablet)

**Technical notes:**
- Use NextAuth.js `CredentialsProvider` with Prisma adapter for session persistence
- Admin layout in `app/admin/layout.tsx` — wraps all admin routes
- Login page at `app/admin/login/page.tsx` — excluded from admin layout (own layout)
- Seed script creates initial admin user with hashed password
- Sidebar: simple `<nav>` with links, active state highlighting

**Dependencies:** 1.x (Foundation — NextAuth, Prisma, DB schema)
**Estimate:** M

---

## 6.2 Admin Dashboard

**Description:** Simple overview page at `/admin` showing key counts and recent activity. Not a complex analytics dashboard — just quick-glance numbers.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Shows count cards: Temujanji hari ini, Tempahan bulan ini, Jumlah Pelanggan, Jumlah Pakej
- [ ] Shows 5 most recent temujanji bookings (nama, tarikh, status)
- [ ] Shows 5 most recent tempahan (nama, tarikh, pakej)
- [ ] Each card links to its respective management page
- [ ] Data fetched server-side (SSR or server component)

**Technical notes:**
- Use server components for data fetching
- Simple Prisma aggregate queries (`count`, `findMany` with `take: 5`)
- Basic card grid layout — no charts or complex visualization needed

**Dependencies:** 6.1
**Estimate:** S

---

## 6.3 Pengurusan Slot Temujanji (Consultation Slot Management)

**Description:** Full CRUD for managing 2-hour consultation time slots. Admin creates available slots; visitors see and book them on the public temujanji page.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] List view: table with columns Tarikh, Masa Mula, Masa Tamat, Status, Tindakan
- [ ] Table is searchable by date and filterable by status (Tersedia/Ditempah)
- [ ] Pagination on the list (default 20 per page)
- [ ] Create form: Tarikh (date picker), Masa Mula (time picker), Masa Tamat (auto-calculated as Mula + 2 hours, editable)
- [ ] Validation: Masa Tamat must be after Masa Mula, no overlapping slots on same date
- [ ] Edit existing slot (only if status is Tersedia)
- [ ] Delete slot (only if status is Tersedia; ditempah slots show warning before delete)
- [ ] Bulk create: option to generate multiple slots for a date (e.g., 10:00-12:00, 14:00-16:00, 16:00-18:00)
- [ ] Status shown as badge: Tersedia (green), Ditempah (amber)

**Technical notes:**
- Use shared DataTable component with server-side pagination
- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/slot-temujanji`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/slot-temujanji/[id]`
- Masa Tamat auto-fills when Masa Mula changes (convenience, not enforced)
- Overlap check in API: query existing slots for same tarikh, check time range intersection

**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — SlotTemujanji)
**Estimate:** M

---

## 6.4 Pengurusan Temujanji (Consultation Booking Management)

**Description:** View and manage all consultation bookings made by visitors. Admin can see pelanggan details, slot info, and notes. Admin can update status or delete bookings.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] List view: table with columns Pelanggan (Nama + No. Telefon), Tarikh, Masa, Nota (truncated), Status, Tindakan
- [ ] Table searchable by pelanggan name or phone number
- [ ] Filterable by date range and status
- [ ] Pagination (default 20 per page)
- [ ] Click row to see full detail view (pelanggan info, slot details, full nota)
- [ ] Admin can delete a temujanji (slot reverts to Tersedia)
- [ ] Admin can manually change slot status if needed
- [ ] No create action from this page — temujanji are created by visitors via public page

**Technical notes:**
- API routes: `GET /api/admin/temujanji`, `DELETE /api/admin/temujanji/[id]`
- Include Pelanggan and SlotTemujanji relations in query
- Deleting temujanji should update the linked SlotTemujanji status back to TERSEDIA (transaction)

**Dependencies:** 6.1, 6.3
**Estimate:** S

---

## 6.5 Pengurusan Slot Tempahan (Venue Booking Slot Management)

**Description:** CRUD for venue booking date slots. Each slot represents a single date the venue is available for booking. Includes a calendar view for visual management.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Calendar view showing all dates with slots (colour-coded: Tersedia green, Ditempah amber/red)
- [ ] Click on empty date to create new slot for that date
- [ ] Click on existing slot to edit or delete
- [ ] Table/list view toggle as alternative to calendar
- [ ] Create form: Tarikh (date picker), Status defaults to Tersedia
- [ ] Bulk create: select date range to create multiple slots at once
- [ ] Delete only Tersedia slots; Ditempah slots require confirmation and cascade warning
- [ ] Month navigation on calendar view

**Technical notes:**
- Reuse calendar grid component (shared with public Kalendar, but interactive version)
- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/slot-tempahan`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/slot-tempahan/[id]`
- Calendar rendering: server-fetch all slots for displayed month, render client-side grid
- Bulk create endpoint accepts `{ startDate, endDate }` and creates one slot per day

**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — SlotTempahan)
**Estimate:** M

---

## 6.6 Pengurusan Tempahan (Venue Booking Management)

**Description:** Manage venue bookings. Admin creates bookings by clicking available slot dates, then fills in booking details. Booked events appear on the public `/kalendar`.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Calendar view of Slot Tempahan; clicking a Tersedia slot opens booking creation form
- [ ] Creation form fields: Nama Tempahan (text), Pakej (select dropdown from existing Pakej), Pelanggan (searchable select from existing Pelanggan)
- [ ] No kategori field — Pakej acts as the category
- [ ] On submit: Tempahan created, SlotTempahan status set to DITEMPAH (transaction)
- [ ] List view: table with columns Nama Tempahan, Tarikh, Pakej, Pelanggan, Tindakan
- [ ] Table searchable by nama tempahan or pelanggan name
- [ ] Filterable by pakej and date range
- [ ] Edit booking: can change nama tempahan, pakej, pelanggan (but not slot date)
- [ ] Delete booking: slot reverts to Tersedia (transaction)
- [ ] Pagination on list view
- [ ] Calendar events colour-coded by Pakej.warna (matches public kalendar)

**Technical notes:**
- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/tempahan`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/tempahan/[id]`
- Creation flow: calendar click -> captures slotTempahanId -> passes to form
- Pakej select: fetch all pakej with `id, namaPakej` for dropdown
- Pelanggan select: searchable/autocomplete dropdown, fetch with debounced search
- Transactions: creating/deleting tempahan must atomically update SlotTempahan status

**Dependencies:** 6.1, 6.5, 6.7, 6.9 (needs Pakej and Pelanggan to exist)
**Estimate:** L

---

## 6.7 Pengurusan Pakej (Package Management)

**Description:** Full CRUD for packages including dynamic tiers (pax + price combos), dynamic sections (title + bullet items), colour picker for calendar coding, and image upload.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] List view: table with columns Nama Pakej, Warna (colour swatch), Bilangan Tier, Gambar (thumbnail), Tindakan
- [ ] Create/Edit form fields:
  - Nama Pakej (text, required)
  - Warna (colour picker input, hex value, required) — used for calendar colour-coding
  - Gambar (image upload, single file, optional)
- [ ] Tiers section: dynamic list, each row has Bilangan Pax (number) + Harga (number, RM formatted)
  - Add tier button, remove tier button per row
  - Minimum 1 tier required
- [ ] Sections section: dynamic list, each row has Tajuk (text) + Items (multi-line or tag input for bullet items)
  - Add section button, remove section button per row
  - Items stored as JSON array of strings
- [ ] Image upload: accept JPG/PNG, max 5MB, stored locally on VPS filesystem (or `/public/uploads/`)
- [ ] Delete pakej: only if no Tempahan references it (show warning if referenced)
- [ ] Colour picker shows preview swatch next to the input

**Technical notes:**
- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/pakej`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/pakej/[id]`
- Image upload: use Next.js API route with `formidable` or `multer`, save to `public/uploads/pakej/`
- Dynamic tiers/sections: managed as nested arrays in form state, sent as JSON
- On save: upsert PakejTier and PakejSection records (delete removed, create new, update existing)
- Colour picker: HTML5 `<input type="color">` is sufficient, or a small library for better UX
- PakejSection items field: store as JSON array in the database column

**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — Pakej, PakejTier, PakejSection)
**Estimate:** L

---

## 6.8 Pengurusan Pakej — Image Upload Utility

**Description:** Shared image upload utility for the Pakej module. Handles file validation, storage, and serving. Kept as a separate concern for reusability.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Upload endpoint accepts multipart form data
- [ ] Validates file type (JPG, PNG only) and size (max 5MB)
- [ ] Generates unique filename (UUID + original extension)
- [ ] Saves to `public/uploads/pakej/` directory
- [ ] Returns the public URL path for the saved image
- [ ] Old image is deleted when a new one is uploaded (update case)
- [ ] Handles missing/corrupt file gracefully with error message

**Technical notes:**
- API route: `POST /api/admin/upload`
- Use `fs.writeFile` for saving (VPS filesystem, not cloud storage)
- Ensure `public/uploads/` is in `.gitignore`
- Return path like `/uploads/pakej/<uuid>.jpg` (Next.js serves from `public/`)

**Dependencies:** 6.1
**Estimate:** S

---

## 6.9 Pengurusan Pelanggan (Customer Management)

**Description:** View and manage customer records. Customers are auto-created when visitors book temujanji, or manually created by admin.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] List view: table with columns Nama, No. Telefon, Nota (truncated), Jumlah Temujanji, Jumlah Tempahan, Tindakan
- [ ] Table searchable by nama or phone number
- [ ] Pagination (default 20 per page)
- [ ] Create form: Nama (required), No. Telefon (required, Malaysian format), Nota (optional)
- [ ] Edit existing pelanggan
- [ ] Delete pelanggan: only if no linked Temujanji or Tempahan (show warning with counts if referenced)
- [ ] Click row to view detail: full info + list of linked temujanji and tempahan

**Technical notes:**
- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/pelanggan`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/pelanggan/[id]`
- Include relation counts in list query: `_count: { temujanji: true, tempahan: true }`
- Phone validation: reuse the same Zod schema pattern from 5.6
- Deduplication: on create, check if phone number already exists and warn (don't block)

**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — Pelanggan)
**Estimate:** M

```
